name: Montjuic Castle
summary: A compact fortress stop with Barcelona views, directly adjacent to the selected Stage 2 Montjuic viewing area.
whyItMatters: The castle is already the Stage 2 viewing reference point, so its cultural value can make waiting on Montjuic more worthwhile if access is open.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Ticket usually required for interior areas; exterior viewpoints may be enough for a short stop.
pros: Essentially on top of the Stage 2 viewing location
Adds Catalan and military-history context to a day otherwise dominated by race logistics
cons: Most vulnerable to race-day access restrictions
Cable car/funicular operations and queues may be disrupted or overloaded

logistics.opening_hours: Official city site should be checked for July 2026 hours and special closure notices.
logistics.reservation_needed: Recommended if entering the castle museum areas.
logistics.distance_from_base: Central Barcelona base dependent; Montjuic hill location.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: At/near the selected Stage 2 Montjuic Castle viewing point.
logistics.transit: Montjuic cable car/funicular, bus, taxi, or walk depending on closures.
logistics.parking: Unknown; avoid assuming private-car access on race day.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Critical: Stage 2 uses repeated Montjuic passages, so official closure checks are required.

timing.best_visit_window: Best as a non-race-day or early-race-day add-on only if the viewing plan already places the group here.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with Poble-sec before climbing or after descending; avoid relying on hilltop food availability during the race.

sources.title: Montjuic Castle official city site
sources.note: Official source for visitor planning, tickets, and special notices.

sources.title: Montjuic Castle - Wikipedia
sources.note: Secondary support for fortress history, municipal status, and access context such as cable car/funicular links.

sources.title: Tour de France Stage 2 official page
sources.note: Supports the race-day relevance of Montjuic and the selected viewing geography.

open_questions: Will the castle itself remain open to visitors on July 5, 2026?
Where will the Stage 2 security perimeter sit relative to the castle entrance?
